column8: Average of FPED MUG Table 8 cup-eq weights for raw berries (145 g), melons except watermelon (170 g), watermelon (150 g), and citrus oranges (185 g); single-figure approximation across the column's three sub-types

FPED MUG Table 8 "All other fruits, raw" cup-eq weights are reported as varied (rounded to nearest 0 or 5 g); 150 g chosen as a typical mid-range value matching apple, banana, and grape cup-equivalents

FPED MUG Table 8: 1 cup-equivalent of 100% fruit juice = 250 g. USDA MyPlate counts 1 cup of 100% juice toward the Fruit Group. Projected onto the modelled "fruits" food group as fresh-fruit-equivalent grams.

FPED MUG Table 9 lists cup-eq weights ranging from 70 g (collards raw) to 180 g (eggplant cooked) depending on the vegetable and preparation. 110 g is a single-figure approximation across the typical US adult mixed-vegetable basket.
Subtract starchy at the same factor used for Total Vegetables so the modelled non-starchy vegetables group is the residual. Starchy mass is added separately below at its FPED-documented density.

FPED MUG p. 35: boiled/canned drained potatoes = 155 g, baked/roasted potatoes = 120 g; FPED MUG Table 9: corn cooked = 165 g. 156 g chosen as a single-figure approximation across the US starchy-vegetable basket.

ASSUMPTION (not a published FPED rule): midpoint of the two FPED MUG Table 10 conversion rules -- 16 g/oz-eq for grain products made of flour (breads, biscuits, pancakes) and 28.35 g/oz-eq for intact grains (rice, pasta, oats, RTE cereals) -- giving (16 + 28.35) / 2 ≈ 22 g/oz-eq. Used as a single factor because FPED's demographic table aggregates flour-based and intact products into one Whole Grains oz-eq column without an external mix breakdown. Picking either extreme materially mis-counts grain mass at the calorie level; 22 g/oz-eq treats the two rules with equal weight.

ASSUMPTION (not a published FPED rule): same 50/50 midpoint of FPED MUG Table 10's 16 g (flour-based) and 28.35 g (intact) rules ≈ 22 g/oz-eq. Refined grain products in the US diet include both bread/baked-goods (16 g) and white rice/pasta (28.35 g); without a published mix split we apply the same midpoint as for whole grains.

FPED MUG Table 11: 1 cup-equivalent of fluid milk = 245 g; same value used for yogurt, buttermilk, dry milk reconstituted, and calcium-fortified soy milk. Cheese contributes via its own cup-eq weight but the column reports the milk-equivalent total. FPED Total Dairy is the low-fat / skim-equivalent fraction (butterfat is stripped into the Solid Fats axis); butter is added separately by the prepare script using FAOSTAT FBS.

FPED MUG Table 13: 1 ounce-equivalent of lean meat = 28.35 g cooked, lean (with up to 2.63 g of solid fat naturally present per oz-eq). Solid fat above this threshold is reported separately in FPED's Solid Fats column.

FPED MUG Table 13 lean-meat definition (28.35 g per oz-eq). Folded into red_meat to match the GDD v09 fold and to keep consumption consistent with FAOSTAT slaughter-volume production accounting; see docs/current_diets.rst.
FPED MUG Table 13: 1 ounce-equivalent of lean poultry = 28.35 g cooked.
FPED MUG Table 13: 1 ounce-equivalent of eggs = 50 g raw or cooked (one large egg).
FPED MUG Table 13: 1 ounce-equivalent of nuts and seeds = ½ oz = 28.35 / 2 = 14.175 g.
FPED MUG Table 13: 1 ounce-equivalent of soy flour, isolate, or concentrate = ½ oz = 14.175 g. Soybean products excluding calcium-fortified soy milk (which is counted under dairy).
FPED MUG Table 13 / Appendix 10: 1 cup-equivalent of cooked legumes = 175 g.
FPED MUG reports the Oils component as gram-equivalents directly; no conversion needed.

USDA SR-Legacy: 1 teaspoon of granulated sugar (sucrose) = 4.2 g. FPED MUG Table 1 uses "tsp eq" as the unit for the Added Sugars component; the 4.2 g/tsp figure is the standard US density for granulated sugar.
